2018-02-26.title: Barnaby Joyce Is Replaced as Australia’s Deputy Prime Minister
2018-02-26.abstract: The conservative Mr. Joyce was felled by scandals including an affair with a staff member. Michael McCormack, the veterans affairs minister, succeeds him.

2018-02-26.title: President Xi’s Strongman Rule Raises New Fears of Hostility and Repression
2018-02-26.abstract: The decision to open a path to a third term for Mr. Xi has injected new uncertainty into international politics.

2018-02-26.title: Rewrite Iran Deal? Europeans Offer a Different Solution: A New Chapter
2018-02-26.abstract: The Europeans are creating an add-on agreement that would extend the existing deal with tighter restrictions, hoping that it will satisfy President Trump.

2018-02-26.title: President Xi Jinping’s Rise in China, as Covered by The Times
2018-02-26.abstract: With a proposed end to term limits, Mr. Xi could be around for much longer. Here’s an overview of how he came to command China.

2018-02-26.title: A Quiet Jerusalem Neighborhood Gets a U.S. Embassy, and a Spotlight
2018-02-26.abstract: The White House is accelerating plans to move diplomatic staff members to Jerusalem, with a small office to be set up in time for the 70th anniversary of the creation of the state of Israel, in May.

2018-02-27.title: Russia Vetoes U.N. Resolution to Pressure Iran Over Yemen Missiles
2018-02-27.abstract: The United States denounced the Russia veto at the U.N. Security Council, which would have otherwise been approved.

2018-02-27.title: Tempers Flare as Panama Hotel Owners Try to Oust Trump Company
2018-02-27.abstract: Officials said they would investigate a dispute between the owners of a Panama City hotel that carries the Trump name and the Trump Organization.

2018-02-27.title: South Africa’s New President Names Allies and Rivals to Cabinet
2018-02-27.abstract: Cyril Ramaphosa appointed well-regarded officials but also some allies of former President Jacob Zuma, as well as a provincial power broker.

2018-02-27.title: New Zealand’s Opposition Party Selects a New Leader
2018-02-27.abstract: The center-right National Party chose Simon Bridges, 41, to lead it as it tries to counter the popularity of Prime Minister Jacinda Ardern.

2018-02-27.title: Success of Irish Trail Shows That Maybe You Can Eat the Scenery
2018-02-27.abstract: The Great Western Greenway, a cycling and walking trail on a disused railway, has helped bring jobs and recreation — whatever the weather.

2018-02-28.title: Pay Me a Pineapple
2018-02-28.abstract: Our audience growth editor is getting married. In her honor, this week’s Australia Diary is about how her talented fiancé, Will Cuming (a musician known as LANKS), became haunted by tales of spiky fruit.

2018-02-28.title: National Identity Is Made Up
2018-02-28.abstract: Nationality feels powerful, especially today. But the idea of identifying with millions of strangers just based on borders is relatively new. We explain why it was invented — and how it changed the world.

2018-02-28.title: National Identity Is Made Up
2018-02-28.abstract: Why do we identify with millions of strangers, just based on borders? In this video from The Interpreter, we explain the myth that built the modern world.

2018-02-28.title: Xi Sets China on a Collision Course With History
2018-02-28.abstract: China kept a half-century of global democratic growth at bay by at least nodding to the importance of institutions and rules. Now what?

2018-02-28.title: Amid Turkish Assault, Kurdish Forces Are Drawn Away From U.S. Fight With ISIS
2018-02-28.abstract: Kurdish fighters in Syria who have been key in the American-led campaign against the Islamic State are peeling off to respond to Turkish attacks.
